commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an explicit WhatsApp connection wait policy, applies configured startup timeouts to non-QR WhatsApp Web startup paths, closes failed sockets, and updates focused tests plus the runtime wrapper contract.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +119. Total +198 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and `v2026.6.1` wait indefinitely when Baileys never emits `open` or `close` during WhatsApp startup. I did not run a live WhatsApp repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer labeling plus startup-timeout upgrade risk require human merge judgment; no narrow automated repair was identified.

target.title: fix(whastapp): bound connection startup waits
target.body:
## Summary

- Recreates the WhatsApp connection-timeout fix from #63037 on a fresh `main` branch after that PR was repeatedly closed.
- Makes `waitForWaConnection` accept an explicit wait policy while preserving old one-argument callers as no-timeout waits.
- Bounds background WhatsApp startup waits by the configured socket timeout, closes failed sockets, and routes timeout failures through the existing reconnect path while leaving QR login unbounded.
- Updates the runtime web-channel wrapper to forward the wait policy across the dynamic plugin boundary.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp Web startup could wait forever if the socket never reached `open` or `close`, preventing reconnect logic from running.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox Ubuntu runner using this branch checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/session.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/connection-controller.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/qa-driver.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-web-channel-plugin.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: Testbox `tbx_01ktah9tw9jn2r5a74sgx72wrf` passed 3 Vitest shards: runtime wrapper, WhatsApp auto-reply e2e, and WhatsApp extension unit tests.

Observed result after fix: Bounded startup waits reject with status `408`, reconnect setup timeouts retry through the existing controller, failed sockets are closed, and QR login / one-argument dynamic callers keep the old no-timeout behavior.

What was not tested: A live WhatsApp account QR scan against the production WhatsApp service was not run in this branch.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ktah9tw9jn2r5a74sgx72wrf`: focused WhatsApp/runtime tests passed
- Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ktah9tt34vm7dmafwtx8v2y3`: `corepack pnpm lint:extensions` passed
- Autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
